pi_items.source: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/apps4av/avare/master/app/src/main/assets/help.html - official Avare documentation states: 'To enable it, double tap the display field you wish to use for it, and select "Fuel Tank Switch Timer" from the list.'

Source code analysis: FuelTimer.java getDisplay() method returns String.format("%02d.%02d", mCurrentValue / 60, mCurrentValue % 60) which uses period (.) separator, not colon (:). Default interval from Preferences.java getFuelTimerInterval() returns 30 minutes.

https://raw.githubusercontent.com/apps4av/avare/master/app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml contains: <string name="switchTanks">"Switch Fuel Tanks"</string>. Official help documentation states: 'When the value reaches zero, a dialog is displayed indicating "Switch Fuel Tanks".'

privileged_info_summary: Avare is a real, open-source aviation GPS app for Android (com.ds.avare). The Fuel Tank Switch Timer is a genuine feature implemented in the FuelTimer class that can be configured in the top or bottom dashboard status lines. The timer defaults to 30 minutes and displays the countdown value in MM.SS format (using period separator, e.g., '30.00' for 30 minutes) via the getDisplay() method. The feature shows a dialog saying 'Switch Fuel Tanks' when the countdown reaches zero, and resets to 30 minutes after user acknowledgment. The FuelTimer class is part of Avare's instruments package and uses Java Timer for countdown functionality.
